t_ch: 好呀，是人家最期待的环节！努力过后就要好好享受，对不对？
t_en: Sure, that's what I've been looking forward to! We deserve to enjoy ourselves after all the hard work.

t_ch: 今天我一定要…嘿嘿……
t_en: Today I'm definitely going to... hehe...

t_ch: 你之前在看什么？
t_en: What were you reading before?

t_ch: 是女孩子的小秘密，就别追问啦。
t_en: A girl's little secret. Don't pry.

t_ch: 为什么不把好看的拿回去？
t_en: Why not take the interesting ones home?

t_ch: 好主意！…不过，那以后再来树庭就没盼头了。
t_en: Great idea! However, that would mean no more reasons to visit the Grove.

t_ch: 这地方就有一点好，想看什么题材的都能找到。
t_en: That's the charm of this place. You can find whatever you're into here.

t_ch: 像咱们三个，恐怕都对所谓学术著作不感兴趣，但还是能在友爱之馆泡上一整天。
t_en: The three of us might not be into scholarly books, but we could still spend an entire day at the Library of Philia.

t_ch: 过去的几个月，你不止一次来到这里，在知识的海洋中打捞最符合你口味的那一小块精华。
t_en: Over the past few months, you've come here more than once, fishing out that small piece of essence from the ocean of knowledge that best suits your taste.

t_ch: 你很庆幸丹恒不在场，这样就不会有人对你的阅读品味评头论足。
t_en: You're quite relieved that Dan Heng isn't around — no one will be there to judge your reading tastes.

t_ch: 眼前的卷轴显示屏上映出一串小字：「非常规读物，借阅后请放回原处，切勿被任何一位树庭教授发现，叹号乘十。」
t_en: The scroll at hand shows a line of small text: "Non-standard reading material. Please return to its original place after borrowing. Do not let any Grove professor discover it — ten exclamation marks!"

t_ch: 看来树庭的学者们在深奥的学术探讨之余，也没忘了自己的小爱好。那么，你要翻阅哪篇读物呢？
t_en: It seems that the scholars of the Grove, amidst their profound academic discussions, haven't forgotten their little hobbies. So, which reading material will you peruse?

t_ch: 《禁忌之恋：树与蝶》
t_en: Forbidden Love: The Tree and the Butterfly

t_ch: …没了？这就没了？
t_en: ...That's it? Nothing else?

t_ch: 这篇爱情故事在最糟糕的部分戛然而止。不知是作者失去了写下去的动力，还是发生了别的紧急情况……
t_en: This love story abruptly ends at its worst moment. Whether the author lost the motivation to continue or some urgent situation occurred remains unknown...

t_ch: …如果是后者，那盗火行者得负全责。
t_en: ...If it were the latter, the Flame Reaver is fully responsible.

t_ch: 《我的教授不可能那么娇羞》
t_en: My Professor Can't Possibly Be This Shy

t_ch: 读完这篇令你三观震颤的小文，你的脑海中只剩下一个画面……
t_en: After finishing this mind-shaking piece, only one image remains in your mind...

t_ch: …列车。瓦尔特。手机。
t_en: ...the Express. Welt. Phone.

t_ch: 对于一位年轻的星核精而言，这样的文学还是太超前了。
t_en: For a young Stellaron host, this type of literature remains far too ahead of its time.

t_ch: 《一拳神王•艾格勒卷》
t_en: One Punch God: Aquila

t_ch: 劲爽文学，你的最爱。
t_en: Invigorating literature, your favorite.

t_ch: 读完此文，你感到全身力量充盈，仿佛作者笔下的一拳神王就是你的化身。
t_en: This article leaves you feeling empowered, as if you are the author's One Punch God incarnate.

t_ch: 天空泰坦艾格勒？呵呵，不过如此。
t_en: Sky Titan Aquila? Heh, that's nothing.

t_ch: 先看到这儿吧…
t_en: Let's stop here...

t_ch: （嗯…不知道白厄和迷迷在看什么呢？）
t_en: (Hmm... I wonder what Phainon and Mem are reading?)

t_ch: （凑过去看看吧。）
t_en: (Let's take a closer look.)
...Hmm?

t_ch: 哈，我就感觉身后有一阵阴风吹过。偷看可不礼貌啊，朋友。
t_en: I thought I felt a draft just now. Peeking isn't polite, my friend.

t_ch: 我就是好奇你在看什么。
t_en: Just curious what you're reading.

t_ch: 那就直接问嘛，又不是什么见不得人的读物。
t_en: You could have just asked. There's nothing embarrassing about it.

t_ch: 在看什么见不得人的东西呢？
t_en: Are you reading something you shouldn't be?

t_ch: 当着你们的面？那我也太不小心了~
t_en: In front of you all? That would be pretty careless.

t_ch: 给你看吧，我在读这个——《古玩鉴定：如何发家致富》。
t_en: Here, look, I'm reading this — Antiques Appraisal: How to Make a Fortune.

t_ch: 你还有这爱好呢？
t_en: Didn't know you were into this.

t_ch: 你不知道？咱们都认识多久了，真让人寒心呐……
t_en: You didn't know? After all this time we've known each other, that pains me...

t_ch: 这个我知道，靠做梦。
t_en: I know how. In your dreams.

t_ch: …你的评论总是这么犀利，让我有点不好接话啊。
t_en: ...Your comments cut deep. It's tough to find a good comeback.

t_ch: 我建议你换个副业。
t_en: Maybe it's time to find a different side gig.

t_ch: …不行，为了这个爱好，我本就不多的家底差不多都填进去了！
t_en: ...No way, I've invested almost all my savings into this hobby!

t_ch: 我认真思考过，阿格莱雅为什么能成为一位优秀的领导者——抛开人格、品行这些看不见摸不着的要素，还有一个重要的原因：她的家底相当殷实！
t_en: I've spent a lot of time pondering how Aglaea became such an effective leader — beyond the intangible qualities like personality and ethics, a major reason is her family wealth!

t_ch: 这就是从小生长在大城邦的优势吧？像我这种偏远乡镇出身的穷孩子就没那么好运了。三分靠打拼，七分天注定……
t_en: That's the perk of growing up in a big city, isn't it? For those of us in the sticks? Luck doesn't come as easily. It's thirty percent grind, seventy percent fate...

t_ch: …咳，一不小心说多了。作为带路的人，最关键的是要德财…德才兼备。
t_en: ...Oops, talked too much. As a leader, you need both integrity and weal — I mean integrity and skill.
Uhh...

t_ch: 耕耘月，幕匿时，宜食稻麦作物，忌垂钓……
t_en: Month of Cultivation, Curtain-Fall Hour, a time to feast on grains, but a poor time for fishing...

t_ch: 不过现在的翁法罗斯，好像也没有可以钓鱼的地方？
t_en: But then again, are there even any spots left to fish in Amphoreus these days?

t_ch: 你在看什么？
t_en: What are you looking at?

t_ch: 你喜欢钓鱼吗？
t_en: Are you into fishing?

t_ch: 迷信迷信，迷迷瞎信。
t_en: Superstitions, superstitions, don't buy into them.

t_ch: 呀，吓我一跳！
t_en: Whoa, you scared me!

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}，这么突然地闯入一个人的世界，可是会让人家手足无措的哦！
t_en: ...{NICKNAME}, bursting into someone's world like that can really catch them off guard!

t_ch: 好啦好啦，不开玩笑了。这里…这里什么都没有！人家去别处看看。
t_en: Well, no more jokes. There's... there's nothing here! I'll go look elsewhere.

t_ch: 这些卡牌的牌面，好精致呀……
t_en: These cards are so detailed and fine...
"Ruler"... "Weaver"... "Traveler"... "Healer"...

t_ch: 唔，但是每一张牌的解读都这么长？要全都背下来吗……
t_en: Hmm, but the interpretation for each card is so long. Do I have to memorize them all?

t_ch: 你在看什么？
t_en: What are you looking at?

t_ch: 「君王」这张和我挺搭。
t_en: "Ruler" fits me pretty well.

t_ch: 灵活运用，不要死记硬背。
t_en: Use them flexibly. Don't just memorize.

t_ch: 啊！{NICKNAME}？嗯…真是巧呢！竟然又见面了，原来我们这么有缘分吗？
t_en: Ah! {NICKNAME}? Hmm... What a coincidence! We meet again. Guess we're fated to be together, huh?

t_ch: 但你一定很忙，对吧？所以人家先不打扰你啦，祝你今天…每一天都过得开心！
t_en: But you must be busy, so I won't bother you for now. Hope your day — every day — is joyful!

t_ch: 你呀…是真的很执着呢。算了算了，人家就是对你这种性格毫无抵抗力……
t_en: You sure are persistent. Alright, alright — I'm totally helpless against your charm...

t_ch: 在聊什么好东西呢？这是…《翁法罗斯占卜全书》？
t_en: What're you guys chatting about? Is this... the Amphoreus Divination Compendium?

t_ch: 你怎么也来啦！
t_en: Why are you here too?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，抱歉，我看你们在边上互相追逐，手里的书瞬间就不香了。
t_en: Haha, sorry, seeing you two chasing each other around, I suddenly found my book not that appealing anymore.

t_ch: 没想到迷迷会对占卜感兴趣…可是，这也不算什么小众爱好，为什么要遮遮掩掩？
t_en: Never thought Mem would be into divination... But it's not exactly a niche hobby, so why all the secrecy?

t_ch: 人家是想准备个惊喜，悄悄学成，吓你们一跳！
t_en: I wanted to set up a surprise — master it in secret and give you all a shock!

t_ch: 可中途被发现了，难免会害羞的嘛。少女柔弱的心弦，可比艾格勒阴晴不定多啦……
t_en: But getting caught halfway through, it's only natural I'd feel shy. A girl's heart is more delicate than Aquila's ever-changing moods...

t_ch: 哈，「惊喜」么…有人也对我说过类似的话，她特别擅长观星和占卜，尤其是用卡牌——喏，就是你在看的这套。
t_en: Ha, a "surprise," huh? Someone else used to tell me the same. She was really good at stargazing and divination, especially with cards — yeah, just like the ones you're looking at.

t_ch: 白厄还有这样的朋友，那你应该对各类占卜术都挺熟悉的？
t_en: Then you must be pretty familiar with all sorts of divination techniques, with a friend like that.

t_ch: 不，一无所知。用卡牌、髀石预测命运，我从小就不怎么相信。
t_en: No, I know nothing about it. I've never really believed in using cards or bones to predict the future.

t_ch: 要是占卜结果里提到的吉兆都是真实的…那我的家乡，哀丽秘榭，也就不会消失了。
t_en: If the good omens foretold by divination were true... my hometown, Aedes Elysiae, wouldn't have disappeared.

t_ch: 别忘了，我们还有缇宁老师。和她从未失准的预言相比，所有占卜术都不过是障眼法，或是预先排练好的诡辩。
t_en: Don't forget, we've got Lady Trinnon. Every other form of divination is just smoke and mirrors or cleverly rehearsed tricks compared to her flawless prophecies.

t_ch: 偶尔信一下也没什么不好。
t_en: A little faith in these things isn't bad sometimes.

t_ch: 应该带着批判的眼光看待…
t_en: It should be approached with a critical eye...

t_ch: 占卜…在人家看来，也许是一种指引心灵的力量。
t_en: To me, divination could be a power that guides our spirits.

t_ch: 命运总在人的前路投下阴影，让他们无法清晰地看见自己的未来。
t_en: Fate always casts a shadow in front of us, clouding our view of the future.

t_ch: 所以人们才需要占卜，只是想给自己一些小小的理由，鼓起跨越那片未知的勇气……
t_en: That's why people turn to divination. It's just to find a small reason to give them courage to face the unknown...

t_ch: 你想得还真深。
t_en: You've really thought about this, haven't you?

t_ch: 毕竟《占卜全书》第一章讲的就是「占卜的意义」，我只是稍微进行了一点延展思考！
t_en: After all, the first chapter of the "Divination Compendium" discusses "the significance of divination." I'm just expanding on that a bit!

t_ch: 万一占卜出来的是坏结果呢？
t_en: What if divination predicts something bad?

t_ch: 就是因为结果有好有坏，占卜才有意义呀。
t_en: Divination is only meaningful when there are both good and bad outcomes.

t_ch: 坏的征兆也是一种指引，至少能勾起人们的警觉，对吧？
t_en: Even bad omens serve as guidance. They can help keep us alert, right?

t_ch: 奇怪…我认识的那个人，她也说过类似的话。回想起来，每次她为我解读卡面，我的心情都会莫名地忐忑…哪怕我并不相信占卜的结果。
t_en: Strange... That person I knew used to say similar things. Looking back, whenever she interpreted a card for me, I couldn't help but feel uneasy. Even though I never believed in the outcomes.

t_ch: 大概是因为她的语气吧？昔涟…她能把一场彻头彻尾的幻梦描绘得栩栩如生，让儿时的我沉浸其中。
t_en: Maybe it was her tone? Cyrene... she could paint a complete fantasy so vividly, it utterly captivated me as a child.

t_ch: 对了。我随身带着她以前用过的一张牌，想看看吗？
t_en: Right. I still carry a card she once used. Do you want to see it?

t_ch: 哇…好精致！人家也好想拥有这样的牌呀。
t_en: Wow... it's so exquisite! I'd love to have a card like that.

t_ch: 喔，经你这么一提醒，还真是！要是能换成其他材质，比如水晶…就更好啦！
t_en: Oh, now that you mention it, yes! It would be even better if it were made of, say... crystal!

t_ch: 说起来，{NICKNAME}，有件事我一直没和你们通气。还记得我们第一次去命运三相殿时的情形吗？当时，欧洛尼斯给我们出的那道谜……
t_en: Speaking of which, {NICKNAME}, there's something I haven't shared with you all. Remember our first trip to the Temple of the Three Fates? The riddle that Oronyx posed to us...

t_ch: …谁在那？！
t_en: ...Who's there!?

t_ch: 你的爪子拿得住吗？
t_en: Those mitts up to the task?

t_ch: 得设计一套迷迷专用牌。
t_en: Then, gotta craft a Mem-specific deck.

t_ch: 你们是……
t_en: You are...

t_ch: 有刺客，快救朕！
t_en: Assassins. Save me!

t_ch: cos服不错啊。
t_en: Nice cosplay.

t_ch: 这些人，来者不善呢……
t_en: These folks are up to no good...

t_ch: 黑色衣装…带着金血装饰的面罩……
t_en: Black clothing... Masks decorated with golden blood...

t_ch: …是「清洗者」？
t_en: ...Are they "Cleaners"?

t_ch: 呵…竟然能直接叫出我们的名字，看来你对翁法罗斯遗落的历史也颇有研究啊，白厄阁下。
t_en: Ha... To directly call us by our name, it seems you, Lord Phainon, have studied the forgotten history of Amphoreus quite thoroughly.

t_ch: 他们是谁？
t_en: Who are they?

t_ch: 洗什么？衣服还是车？
t_en: What's being cleaned? Clothes or cars?

t_ch: 你的朋友似乎对我们很感兴趣。和{F#她}{M#他}解释一下吧，白厄阁下？出于礼节……
t_en: Your friend seems quite interested in us. Perhaps you could explain, Lord Phainon? It would be the polite thing...

t_ch: …可不能让贵客不明不白地离去。
t_en: ...We can't let our esteemed guests pass away without understanding.

t_ch: …黄金战争时期，为了对抗黄金裔，多个城邦在地下集结了一支刺客部队，取名「清洗者」。
t_en: During the Chrysos War, to combat the Chrysos Heirs, several cities formed an underground assassin squad called "the Cleaners."

t_ch: 他们的职责便是将流着金血的人从世上抹去。暗杀、投毒、政治迫害…为了这个目标，他们无所不用其极。
t_en: Their task was to erase anyone with golden blood from existence. Assassination, poisoning, political persecution... They would do anything to achieve their objective.

t_ch: 这些史料的立场似乎有些偏颇，为了丑化先辈们的事业，把黄金裔滥用神力、悍然掀起战乱的滔天罪行一笔带过……
t_en: This historical perspective seems biased, glossing over the Chrysos Heirs' abuse of divine powers and their blatant initiation of war, in order to defame our predecessors...

t_ch: 能劳烦你把这段野史作者的姓名透露给我么，白厄阁下？我可不希望奥赫玛纯真的孩子们，被这家伙的春秋笔法荼毒啊。
t_en: Could you disclose the name of the author behind this popular legend, Lord Phainon? I'd hate for the innocent children of Okhema to be tainted by such slanted narratives.

t_ch: 你们本该老老实实呆在历史的积尘里。公民大会已再度为黄金裔的使命赋予了正当性，至于你们……
t_en: You should have stayed buried in the dust of history. The citizens' assembly has once again legitimized the mission of the Chrysos Heirs, but as for you...

t_ch: 不会有一位公民把票投给你们这群阴影里的刽子手。
t_en: Not one citizen would cast their vote for you executioners lurking in the shadows.

t_ch: 那又如何？我们早已不再奢求能得到理解……
t_en: What does it matter? We've long since stopped expecting to be understood...

t_ch: 他们和平日子过惯了…早就忘了让黄金裔世代掌权，曾经酿出何等祸根！
t_en: People have become too accustomed to peace... and they've forgotten the disasters that arose from allowing generations of the Chrysos Heirs to rule!

t_ch: 胡搅蛮缠。你们远赴树庭，就是为了逞口舌之快么？
t_en: Complete nonsense. Did you really travel all the way to the Grove just to have an argument?

t_ch: 你很爽快，白厄阁下，我喜欢跟你这样的人打交道。
t_en: I enjoy dealing with people as straightforward as you, Lord Phainon.

t_ch: 那想必你也能猜到吧？我们出现在诸位面前的理由……
t_en: You've probably figured out why we've come before you...

t_ch: 就这么几个人，打发谁呢？
t_en: Just a handful of you here. Who do you think you're fighting?

t_ch: 要战便战，少废话！
t_en: If it's a fight you want, let's get on with it — enough chit-chat!

t_ch: 冷静，{NICKNAME}。他们敢走出阴影，就表示手中掌握了足以威慑我们的筹码。
t_en: Keep your cool, {NICKNAME}. They've come out of the shadows, which suggests they have something up their sleeves.

t_ch: 说说看吧，你们手里究竟藏着什么牌。但是，千万要小心组织语言，别说错话。
t_en: Alright, let's hear it — what are you hiding? But watch your language, and don't say anything you might regret.

t_ch: 我的肩膀很酸痛，剑也有些钝了…但要削掉你们的舌头？那还绰绰有余。
t_en: My shoulders might be sore and my sword a tad dull... but it's still enough to slice off your tongues.

t_ch: 大家小心点。
t_en: Eyes peeled, everyone.
Ah...

t_ch: 怎么样，小风堇？这里很适合吧？
t_en: How's it going, Cinny? This place fits, doesn't it?

t_ch: 地方虽然小了些，但采光堪称完美！
t_en: It's a bit small, but the lighting is perfect!

t_ch: 谢谢你，缇宝老师！如此一来，昏光庭院就能在奥赫玛获得新生啦。
t_en: Thank you, Lady Tribbie! With this, the Twilight Courtyard can be reborn in Okhema.

t_ch: 你也喜欢这里吗，小伊卡？
t_en: Do you like it here too, Little Ica?

t_ch: 嘟…嘟嘟！
t_en: Doot... Doot-Doot!

t_ch: 嘿嘿…这里这么亮堂，你怕不是该给它换个名字啦？比如…「明光庭院」，如何？
t_en: Hehe... It's so bright here, maybe you should rename it? How about "Luminous Courtyard"?

t_ch: 缇宝老师说得有道理。不过…「昏光庭院」这个名字承载了天空后裔的悠久历史，我还是想让它延续下去。
t_en: Lady Tribbie makes a good point. But... "Twilight Courtyard" carries the long history of the descendants of the sky, and I'd like it to continue.

t_ch: 对哦…传说里是怎么记载的来着？唔……
t_en: Right... How does the legend go again? Hmm...

t_ch: 嘿…缇宝老师也会忘记事情吗？
t_en: Heh... Even Lady Tribbie forgets things...

t_ch: …我、*我们*只是太久没备课了，稍微有点生疏了而已！给*我们*点时间，一定能想起来！
t_en: ...We... We just haven't prepared lessons in a while now, so we're a bit rusty! Just give us some time, and we'll remember!

t_ch: …啊，这不就想起来啦！《悬锋与苍穹》，第三卷第五节——
t_en: ...Ah, now we remember! Kremnos and the Firmament, Volume 3, Section 5 —

t_ch: 「<color=#dbc291ff>阳雷骑士塞涅俄丝击落晨昏之眼</color>，标志着尼卡多利与艾格勒之间以百年为单位的漫长神战落下帷幕……」
t_en: "<color=#dbc291ff>With Seliose, the Daythunder Knight, striking down the Eye of Twilight</color>, the divine war between Nikador and Aquila, which spanned centuries, came to an end..."

t_ch: 「此后…天空之民逐渐式微，诸多天空堡垒遭到废弃，他们的后裔开始于大地之上扎根求生……」
t_en: "Thereafter... the Skyfolk gradually declined, numerous sky fortresses were abandoned, and their descendants began to establish themselves on the land..."

t_ch: 「最后，继承了阳雷骑士塞涅俄丝血脉的一族被瑟希斯的学者们接纳，并在树庭建立起了昏光庭院……」
t_en: "Finally, those descended from Seliose the Daythunder Knight were welcomed by the scholars under Cerces and established the Twilight Courtyard in the Grove..."

t_ch: 唔，总感觉史书里的记载，和*我们*记忆中的来龙去脉稍有出入呢…但当时的细节，*我们*也有些记不清了。
t_en: Hmm, the historical records seem slightly at odds with our memories... but the details from that time have become somewhat hazy to us.

t_ch: 差点忘了…对于缇宝老师来说，历史不止是书卷上的文字，也是你亲身经历过的时代呀。
t_en: Oh, I forgot... For Lady Tribbie, history is more than just words on a page — it's something you've personally lived through.

t_ch: 嘟！嘟嘟，嘟！
t_en: Doot! Doot-Doot, Doot!

t_ch: 嘻，小伊卡还是很喜欢听这些传说故事呢。你肯定也想象着，自己有朝一日能和英雄塞涅俄丝身边的翼兽一样威风吧？
t_en: Heh, Little Ica loves listening to the stories from the legends. I'm sure you're also imagining, one day, you'll be as awe-inspiring as the winged beasts by Seliose's side?

t_ch: 会有那么一天的，我相信！只要你好好长大……
t_en: That day will come! I believe it! Just keep growing...

t_ch: …啊，克拉特鲁斯阁下？
t_en: ...Ah, Lord Krateros?

t_ch: 风堇姑娘……
t_en: Miss Hyacine...

t_ch: …还有缇宝大人，你们没事便好。我听公民说你们在附近，就顺便前来看看。
t_en: ...And Lady Tribbie, good to see you're doing alright. I heard from the citizens you were nearby and came to check.

t_ch: 没…事？发生什么了嘛，克拉特鲁斯？
t_en: Doing... alright? What's going on, Krateros?

t_ch: 市集又发生了恶性治安事件，有店铺遭到了砸抢。已经是这周内的第三次了。
t_en: There's been another violent incident at the market. A shop was smashed and looted. It's the third time this week.

t_ch: 这…怎么会这样？我一直以为，有阿格莱雅大人在，奥赫玛一直都很安定祥和……
t_en: How can this be? I always thought that with Lady Aglaea here, Okhema would remain peaceful...

t_ch: 过去的确如此。但近些日子…恕我直言，她的感官恐怕没有从前那么敏锐了。
t_en: That was true in the past. But these days... forgive my bluntness, her senses may not be as sharp as they once were.

t_ch: 阿雅……
t_en: Agy...

t_ch: 依我之见，恐怕是有用心险恶之人觉察到了金线的松动，于是开始策划阴谋，乘隙启衅。
t_en: In my opinion, malevolent forces have noticed the slack in the golden threads and are now plotting to exploit the opportunity.

t_ch: 明明公民大会刚刚结束不久…为什么还会有人想要搞破坏？
t_en: The citizens' assembly just wrapped up... Why is there still unrest?

t_ch: 黄金裔只以一票胜出，缇宝大人。根据我征战多年的经验，惨胜和动荡往往形影不离。
t_en: The Chrysos Heirs won by only one vote, Lady Tribbie. From my years of experience, a narrow victory often walks hand-in-hand with unrest.

t_ch: …出大事了！风堇小姐…出大事了！
t_en: ...Trouble! Miss Hyacine... trouble!

t_ch: …克莱门汀？你…别着急，快先喘口气！
t_en: ...Clementine? Take a breath... What's going on?

t_ch: 三、三位大人！云石天宫，出…出大事了！
t_en: Please! Something terrible has happened at Marmoreal Palace!

t_ch: 什么大事，医师？冷静下来，复述一下情况！
t_en: What is it, doctor? Calm yourself and talk us through!

t_ch: 阿格莱雅大人……
t_en: Lady Aglaea...

t_ch: 出事的…是阿格莱雅大人！
t_en: It's... it's Lady Aglaea who's in trouble!

t_ch: 片刻之前，云石天宫……
t_en: Moments ago in Marmoreal Palace...

t_ch: 我还记得，这座能俯瞰整座天宫的浴场，是元老院于九百年前主张兴建的。
t_en: I still remember, this bath that overlooks the entire Palace was built by the Council of Elders nine hundred years ago.

t_ch: 建立它的目的，原本是为了纪念那第一次逐火之旅，为了追忆于千年前为翁法罗斯做出重大牺牲的英雄们。
t_en: Its purpose was to commemorate the first Flame-Chase Journey, and to remember the heroes who made great sacrifices for Amphoreus a millennium ago.

t_ch: 规矩也自那时定下。除了受到「金织」邀请的宾客，他人不得擅闯此处。
t_en: Rules were set then. No one was allowed to enter, apart from guests invited by the Goldweaver.

t_ch: 但，我猜…你们现在已经无意假装规行矩步了，对么？凯妮斯阁下。
t_en: But, I guess... you're no longer pretending to follow the rules, are you, Lady Caenis?

t_ch: 阿格莱雅，呵，阿格莱雅……
t_en: Aglaea, Aglaea...

t_ch: 你这千年不死的妖女，披着神明的皮在人间惑众…一直以来，我们都对你太仁慈了。
t_en: You ageless witch, hiding behind a divine facade to deceive mortals... We've been far too kind with you.

t_ch: 仁慈？何必用这个惺惺作态的词，来掩饰你们的……
t_en: Kind? There's no need to use such a word to cover up your...

t_ch: 「无能」。
t_en: Incompetence.

t_ch: 阿那克萨戈拉斯令各位在公民面前颜面尽失。若你们还心存廉耻，就该在幕匿时纵身跃下黎明云崖，以保全自己最后一丝尊严。
t_en: Anaxagoras humiliated all of you before the citizens. If you still had any shred of shame, you'd throw yourselves from Dawncloud at the Curtain-Fall Hour to salvage the last fragments of your dignity.

t_ch: 半神，何不以池水作镜，好好看看你如今的恶毒相？
t_en: Demigod, why not gaze into the water's reflection and see the wretched face you wear now?

t_ch: 如今你不但痴盲，而且昏聩…「清洗者」就是为了这个时刻诞生的。当一切秩序、规矩和法理都无法制约你们，那就该轮到匕首来审判了。
t_en: These days you're not only blind but also deaf... The "Cleaners" were born for this moment. When all orders, rules, and laws fail to restrain you, it's time for the dagger to pass judgment.

t_ch: 若真如此急不择途，你们倒不妨试试看。
t_en: If you're in such a hurry, give it a try.

t_ch: 哼…哼哼…与半神正面较量？我们没蠢到那个地步。
t_en: Hmph... going toe-to-toe with a demigod? We're not that foolish.

t_ch: 匕首并非抵在你的脖颈上，而是……
t_en: The dagger isn't at your throat, but...

t_ch: 你对我最严峻的指控便是人性将尽，你还指责我对人间悲欢的关切皆是假意。
t_en: Your gravest accusation is that my humanity is fading, and you claim my concern for mortal joys and sorrows is nothing but a facade.

t_ch: 如果指控成立…你凭什么觉得这些平民的性命能令我妥协？
t_en: If that accusation holds... what makes you think the lives of these civilians will sway me in the slightest?

t_ch: 无妨，那我就再费些口舌，戳穿你这半神的面具吧。
t_en: Fine. I'll gladly use a few more words to shatter that demigod facade of yours.

t_ch: 我早说了，如今你不但痴盲，而且昏聩。近些日子，你对奥赫玛的掌控变弱了。
t_en: Like I said, you're not only blind but deaf too. Lately, your control over Okhema has weakened.

t_ch: 你看不到我们刻意在市集安排的行窃，也没能监听到在大地兽工坊发生的接头。对于在你眼皮底下酝酿着的阳谋，你无动于衷。
t_en: You failed to notice the thefts we arranged in the market, nor did you catch wind of the meetings happening in the Dromas Workshop. Plots are unfolding right under your nose, yet you remain indifferent.

t_ch: 人性将尽？呵，阿格莱雅，你不妨用你那引以为傲的金线读读我的想法，看看我们精心给你准备的礼物，能不能让一尊冰冷的半神动容？
t_en: Humanity fading? Ha, Aglaea, why not use your precious golden thread to peer into my thoughts? See if the gift we've so meticulously prepared for you can move that cold demigod heart of yours.

t_ch: 此情此景，我只怨自己还不够冷漠。因为你的邪恶…无药可医。
t_en: In this moment, I only regret not being more ruthless. Because your evil... is beyond cure.

t_ch: 终于读出我的想法了？哈…你果真变得迟钝了。为了让你清晰地看见，我可是一直尽力在脑子里维持那个画面啊。
t_en: You finally figured it out? Ha... you have grown dull. I've been holding that image in my mind this whole time to make sure you could see it clearly.

t_ch: （这块髀石护身符，就送给姐姐吧。希望你每天都能有好运气，将来也不要被任何人欺负！）
t_en: (I want you to have this knucklebone talisman, big sis. I hope it brings you luck every day and keeps you safe from bullies!)

t_ch: 那个无法发声或聆听的女孩…假如她又*不小心*失去了双眼，会发生什么？
t_en: That girl who can't speak or hear... What if she accidentally lost her eyes too?

t_ch: 失去光明的滋味，你比谁都清楚。想象一下，千年以前，倘若年少失明的你又被剥夺了声带和听觉——你还能成为现在这个阿格莱雅吗？
t_en: You know better than anyone what it feels like to lose your sight. Imagine if a thousand years ago, you, already blind, had also lost your voice and hearing in your youth — would you still have become the Aglaea you are today?

t_ch: 瞧你那残缺又乞怜的眼神…真是让人扫兴，我们高估了你的手腕。
t_en: Look at those broken, pleading eyes... how disappointing. We overestimated your cunning.

t_ch: 若此计不成，我们本还准备了数十样精彩的临别赠礼…半神，你要听听么？
t_en: If this plan fails, we still have dozens of splendid parting gifts prepared... Demigod, would you like to hear about them?

t_ch: …我曾以为，我还能争取到更多时间。
t_en: ...I thought I could buy some more time.

t_ch: 一千年的时光…我见证了十位泰坦的殒落。从最初的娇蛮无知，再到摸索着为众人引路……
t_en: In these one thousand years... I've witnessed the fall of ten Titans. From my naive arrogance at the beginning to slowly learning how to guide others...

t_ch: 现在…该放手了。我该庆幸…庆幸自己还是等来了这一刻——<color=#dbc291ff>能将我的离去筑成众人的长阶。</color>
t_en: Now... it's time to let go. I should be grateful... grateful that I've reached this moment — <color=#dbc291ff>to let my departure become a path for others to follow.</color>

t_ch: …我做得够吗？<color=#dbc291ff>若我就此拥抱那预言，在温热的池水中睡去……</color>
t_en: ...Have I done enough? <color=#dbc291ff>If I embrace that prophecy now and sink into the warmth of these waters...</color>

t_ch: …远行的风儿会为我驻足吗，赛法利娅？
t_en: ...will the wandering wind stop to mourn me, Cifera?

t_ch: 到了！上次那块石板在哪？是这里，还是这里……
t_en: Here we are! Where was that slate last time? Was it here, or maybe over here...

t_ch: 人家先去玩啦！现在是自由活动时间，你也要放松放松哦。
t_en: I'm off to have some fun! It's free time now, so you should relax too.

t_ch: （迷迷的样子好奇怪，它到底在翻什么书？）
t_en: (Something's odd about Mem. What are they flipping through?)

t_ch: （好可疑，完全不像平日的它……）
t_en: (So suspicious, not like their usual self...)

t_ch: 千年前，云石市集……
t_en: A millennium ago at Marmoreal Market...

t_ch: 裁缝女，我如约来了！瞧这满满一箱金银财宝，我敢打赌，准有你没见过的稀罕玩意！
t_en: Seamstress, I've come as promised! Look at this box full of gold and silver treasures. I bet there's stuff here you've never seen!

t_ch: 来吧，按照之前说好的，我要买下你这里最值钱的衣服！
t_en: Come on, as we agreed, I want to buy the most valuable clothes you have!

t_ch: 你这毛贼，礼数不多胆量不小，竟敢穿着这般凋敝的衣衫闯进我的织坊？
t_en: Little thief, you've got some nerve, barging into my shop dressed in those tattered rags!

t_ch: 你都带来了些什么，让我看看吧。
t_en: What have you brought? Let me see.

t_ch: 喏，尽管看！都是一等一的好货——你看这颗蓝宝石，简直比法吉娜的眼珠子还要蓝哪！
t_en: Here, take a look! Only the finest goods — see this sapphire? It's even more blue than Phagousa's eyes!
Ah...

t_ch: 何等华贵的光泽…这般成色的海洋之泪，我只在母亲的宝库中见过……
t_en: Such exquisite luster... I've only seen an Ocean's Tear of this quality in my mother's treasury...

t_ch: …什么啊，原来你以前见过？我还以为能让你大吃一惊呢。嘁，扫兴。
t_en: ...Wait, you've seen one before? And here I thought I'd leave you speechless. Ugh, what a letdown.

t_ch: 久远的记忆罢了。我的家产早就被大火焚尽，这间织坊就是我白手起家的成果。
t_en: It's just an old memory. My family estate all burned to ashes long ago. This workshop is what I built from scratch.

t_ch: 嚯，重振家业的大小姐，多么令人钦佩…呵，少开玩笑了，至少你确实享受过那些荣华富贵。
t_en: Wow, a daughter rebuilding her family business — how admirable... Heh, enough with the jokes. At least you did get to enjoy those luxuries.

t_ch: 呵…无礼的野猫。
t_en: Heh... Mind your manners, kitty.

t_ch: 我问你，赛法利娅——你是自何处，又是以何种手段得到这些财宝的？
t_en: Tell me, Cifera, where did you come from, and how did you acquire these treasures?

t_ch: 哈，我光明正大和人打赌赢来的。怎么，你怀疑我不成？
t_en: Ha, I won them fair and square in a bet. What, do you doubt me?

t_ch: 说话呀，裁缝女！给个准信，这些宝贝你收还是不收？
t_en: Speak up, seamstress! Give me a straight answer. Do you want these or not?

t_ch: 真是怪异，你明明就在我的面前扯谎……
t_en: Strange, you're clearly lying to my face...

t_ch: 但我的金线…却没有丝毫震颤。
t_en: But my golden thread... doesn't tremble at all.

t_ch: 那是因为我字字实话，压根就没骗人。
t_en: That's because I'm not lying. I've been telling the truth from the start.

t_ch: 果真如此么？但你脸上的伤痕和泥印，却在给我讲述另外一个故事。
t_en: Is that so? But the scars and mud stains on your face tell another story.

t_ch: 你…哎呀，做个生意，哪来那么多废话！你放心，我没留下任何把柄，不会有人来找你麻烦的。
t_en: ...Oh come on, can't we just do business without all this chatter? Don't worry, I didn't leave any evidence, so no one will bother you.

t_ch: 你如此擅长说谎，我又如何能确定这话是否属实？
t_en: How can I trust a word you say when you're so skilled at lying?

t_ch: …跟你们这些有钱人打交道可真麻烦，浪费我的时间！算了，你不收，别处还有的是人……
t_en: ...Dealing with you rich people is such a pain, always wasting my time! Fine, if you don't want it, there are plenty of others who do...

t_ch: …等等。
t_en: ...Hold on.

t_ch: 你的财宝，我收下了。但仅凭这些财宝，尚不足以买下「金织」最昂贵的衣装。
t_en: Your treasure — I'll accept it. But these alone aren't enough to purchase the Goldweaver's finest clothes.

t_ch: 话虽如此，我倒有一件和你足够般配的服饰。
t_en: That said, I do have something that would suit you perfectly.

t_ch: 哦？是什么好东西？
t_en: Oh? What do you have in mind?

t_ch: 看吧，贴近些。
t_en: Look. Come a bit closer.

t_ch: 喔！这是…金光闪闪的，真漂亮啊！
t_en: Oh! It's... shimmering gold, absolutely stunning!

t_ch: 这双金靴，算得上「金织」的至宝之一。尽管价值比不上最名贵的华服，却蕴含着神奇的力量。
t_en: These golden boots are the Goldweaver's greatest treasures. Though not as valuable as the most precious gowns, they hold a magical power.

t_ch: 传说{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}曾祝福过这双靴子，令所有者能以神速疾行，且不会激起任何响动……
t_en: Legend has it that {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} blessed these boots, allowing anyone who wears them to move swiftly and silently...

t_ch: 穿上它吧，赛法利娅，下次别再被抓住了。你姣好的容颜值得更为悉心的保护。
t_en: Put them on, Cifera, and don't get caught again. A face as lovely as yours deserves better protection.

t_ch: 哼，你们这些有钱人就是喜欢多管闲事！那我可拿走了，你再想反悔就晚了！
t_en: Hmph, you rich folks just love sticking your noses in everything, don't you? Well, I'm taking these now, so don't get any second thoughts!

t_ch: 唔…嗯……
t_en: Uhh... hmm...

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 那个…有没有小一码的？这双对我来说也太大了……
t_en: Uh... do you have a smaller size? These are way too big for me...

t_ch: 呵…那就等你再长大些后穿吧。
t_en: ...Well, I suppose you'll have to grow into them.

t_ch: …假的！假的！
t_en: ...Fake! Fake!

t_ch: …你就非得在我讲故事的中途打岔吗？假的，什么假的？
t_en: ...Do you have to interrupt me in the middle of my story? Fake? What's fake?

t_ch: 你那双靴子，根本就没有扎格列斯的神力！
t_en: Those boots definitely don't hold Zagreus's divine power!

t_ch: 翁法罗斯最会撒谎的女人，也被那个阿格莱雅给骗了，桀桀桀！
t_en: You're Amphoreus's most cunning liar, and even you were fooled by Aglaea — hehehe!

t_ch: 嘴欠的家伙，小心我把你丢进冥河里喂鱼！
t_en: Watch your mouth, or I'll feed you to the fish in the River of Souls!

t_ch: 你不是好奇我是怎么跟那个裁缝女扯上关系的吗？现在你知道了。满意了？
t_en: Weren't you curious how I got tangled up with that seamstress? Well, now you know. Satisfied?

t_ch: 桀桀…没想到是这么简朴的故事，真是让人大失所望啊。
t_en: Hehe... I didn't expect such a simple story. How disappointing.

t_ch: 不过，呃…大姐头，也有一种可能，阿格莱雅不是故意想要骗你的……
t_en: But, uh... Boss Lady, there's a chance Aglaea wasn't trying to trick you...
Huh?

t_ch: 你…刚才是在替她说话？
t_en: ...You're defending her?

t_ch: 呃…没有没有！我只是觉得，这个故事那么感人，可不能被一句假话给毁了呀——假如我有人的泪腺，现在肯定已经泪流满面了吧！
t_en: Uh... no, no! I just think the story's so moving... it'd be a shame to ruin it with a lie. If I could cry, I'd be in tears right now!

t_ch: 呵！多亏你还有油嘴滑舌这项技能，不然早被抓去给人当看门的宅灵了。
t_en: Ha! Good thing you've still got a sharp tongue, or you'd have been stuck guarding someone's front door ages ago.

t_ch: 闲扯就到这儿为止，还是抬头看看眼前这座斯缇科西亚城吧——瞧瞧，它多漂亮呀！
t_en: Enough chit-chat. Let's take a look at this place. The city of Styxia — look how beautiful she is!

t_ch: 让我来好好闻一闻，这空气里的味道……
t_en: Let me take a good sniff of this air...

t_ch: 嗅…嗅嗅……
t_en: Sniff... sniff...
Prrrrrr~~

t_ch: 这空气里…到处都弥漫着宝藏的气息！美妙，真是太美妙了！
t_en: This air... it's filled with the scent of treasure everywhere! Wonderful, just wonderful!

t_ch: 多亏了遐蝶跟小灰子，这古城的结界才能被驱散呀——回头想想，我这个忙帮得可太值了~
t_en: Thanks to Castorice and Gray Mystery, the ancient city's barrier was finally dispelled. Now that I think about it, helping them was well worth it.

t_ch: 寻宝时间到了，小巴子！带路吧，找到的好东西咱们八二分成。
t_en: Time for treasure hunting, Bartho! Lead the way, and whatever we find, we'll split <unbreak>80</unbreak>-<unbreak>20</unbreak>.

t_ch: 呃，那个，我有一个，小提议？不如，我们，七三……
t_en: Uh, well, I have a suggestion... How about <unbreak>70</unbreak>-<unbreak>30</unbreak> instead?

t_ch: …嚯，仔细听，你听见了嘛？冥河里的噬骨鱼，肚子正饿得咕噜咕噜叫哪？
t_en: ...Listen closely. Do you hear that? The gnawfish in the River of Souls are growling with hunger...

t_ch: …八二，就八二！你可真是太公平啦，赛飞儿大姐头！
t_en: ...<unbreak>80</unbreak>-<unbreak>20</unbreak> it is! How generous you are, Boss Lady Cipher!

t_ch: …话说回来，这破地方…怎么到处都是水啊？湿漉漉、冰冰凉，讨厌，难受，不喜欢……
t_en: Anyway... What's with all this water? It's damp, cold, and miserable here. I hate it...

t_ch: 桀桀桀，堂堂「诡计」的半神，没想到竟然会对水过敏哇？
t_en: Hehe, the mighty demigod of Trickery... uneasy around water?

t_ch: 你懂什么！多洛斯人个个多才多艺，唯独不太擅长游泳而已。
t_en: You wouldn't understand! The people of Dolos are talented in many ways, just... not in swimming.

t_ch: 有本事就和我比跑步啊？长跑、短跑、转着圈跑，随便挑！
t_en: Why don't we have a race? Long-distance, sprint, or laps — take your pick!

t_ch: 桀桀桀，你怎么急了呀？消消气，消消气…没想到天不怕地不怕的赛飞儿大姐头，居然会怕水呀！
t_en: Hehe, what's got you so worked up? Relax, relax... Who would've thought the fearless Lady Cipher was afraid of water!

t_ch: 猫儿最讨厌沾水——这有什么不好理解的？
t_en: Cats hate getting wet. What's so hard to understand about that?

t_ch: 赶紧想办法让我们过去，否则我就要重新考虑下分成的问题了！
t_en: Now hurry up and figure out a way across, or I might have to reconsider our split!

t_ch: 呃…别着急呀，让我观望观望……
t_en: Uh... don't rush me. Let me take a look around...

t_ch: 有了——就用那玩意吧！跟我来，大姐头！
t_en: Got it — let's use that thing! Come on! Follow me, Boss Lady!

t_ch: （哼…破绽百出啊。）
t_en: (Hmph... Not a good imitation.)

t_ch: （将计就计…就先陪你玩一会儿吧，<color=#dbc291ff>「巴特鲁斯」</color>。）
t_en: (I'll play along... Let's see where this goes, <color=#dbc291ff>"Bartholos."</color>)

t_ch: 桀桀桀，有趣，真有趣……
t_en: Hehe, interesting, very interesting...

t_ch: 这装置…是欧洛尼斯的祭司设计的玩具吧？你能玩明白吗？
t_en: Was this device designed by priests of Oronyx? Do you think you can handle it?

t_ch: 可别小看我啊，大姐头！你就找个干燥的角落好好等着吧，桀桀桀……
t_en: Don't underestimate me, Boss! Just find a cozy, dry corner and wait for me, hehe...

t_ch: 大功告成！这下你就不用担心沾湿尾巴咯。
t_en: Mission accomplished! Now you don't have to worry about your tail getting wet.

t_ch: 不赖嘛。不过我警告你，要是这玩意在我们蹚水到一半的时候失效……
t_en: Not bad. But just a warning — if this thing fails while we're halfway through the water...

t_ch: 没有的事，放心吧！为了那两成的赃…宝贝，我也绝对把您伺候好！
t_en: Don't worry. Trust me! For that sweet <unbreak>20%</unbreak> cut of the loot, I'll make sure you're taken care of!

t_ch: 对，就是这儿——
t_en: Yes, right here —

t_ch: 还愣着干嘛？快开挖呀！
t_en: What are you waiting for? Start digging!

t_ch: 桀桀桀，遵命！看我的吧——
t_en: Roger that! Watch me —

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯开启了挖掘模式，找出了藏于地底的宝藏……
t_en: Bartholos activated excavation mode, unearthing the treasure buried deep underground...

t_ch: 哇…好大一个宝箱！让开让开，我要亲手把它撬开！
t_en: Wow... that's a huge chest! Move over. I want to open it myself!

t_ch: 嚯…嚯嚯，大丰收啊~了不得，真难想象这斯缇科西亚人以前过的是什么神仙日子！
t_en: Wow, wow, a massive haul! Unbelievable, it's hard to imagine the kind of luxurious life the people of Styxia once led!

t_ch: 不愧是法吉娜信徒的地盘，这满满一箱都是拿海洋之泪制成的饰品…喏——拿去！这个赏你，看看合不合适。
t_en: As expected for a realm of Phagousa's disciples. This entire chest is filled with ornaments made from Ocean's Tears... Here — take this, and see if it suits you.

t_ch: 桀桀桀，谢大姐头赏赐，谢谢！
t_en: Hehe, thanks for the gift, Boss! Much appreciated!

t_ch: 哎呀，瞧瞧这紫色水晶的透亮程度…正好跟我的肤色绝配！赛飞儿大姐头，你果真是全翁法罗斯审美最顶级的侠盗！
t_en: Look at the clarity of this purple crystal... It matches my skin tone perfectly! Cipher, Cipher, you truly have the most refined taste of all the thieves in Amphoreus!

t_ch: 桀桀桀，快让我戴上试试…嘢？
t_en: Hehe, let me try this on... Huh?

t_ch: 咦？！这、这是…我的宝贝呢？！
t_en: Huh!? Where... where is... my treasure!?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈——
t_en: Hahahaha —

t_ch: 咱俩都认识多久了？没想到你居然还会上这种当！
t_en: How long have we known each other? I can't believe you still fall for this!

t_ch: 呃…这，这是……
t_en: Uh... this, this is...

t_ch: …哎呀，我怎么能把这事忘了？
t_en: ...Oh, how could I have forgotten about this?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「人人信以为实，谎言即可成真」</color>——多方便的能力啊！
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"All believe it as true, and a lie becomes reality."</color> What a convenient ability!

t_ch: 这就是「诡计」半神的神赐，比什么金线、魔法传送门之类的实用多了，是吧？
t_en: The demigod of Trickery's divine gift, far more practical than any golden thread or teleporter!

t_ch: 好了好了，不耍你了。真品给你——接着带路吧。
t_en: Alright, enough fooling around. Here's the real thing. Lead the way.

t_ch: 大姐头威武！我已经定位到下一处宝藏了，即刻开始导航…桀桀桀……
t_en: Cipher rules! I've already pinpointed the next trove of treasure. Starting navigation... hehehe...

t_ch: 这玩意一启动，发出的动静几里外都能听得清清楚楚——真是蠢得没边啦！也不知道当时……
t_en: Once activated, this thing makes a racket that can be heard for miles — just too stupid! What was—...

t_ch: 咳…也不知道泰坦发明它的时候是怎么想的，是吧？
t_en: Ahem... Seriously, what was the Titan thinking when they came up with this thing?

t_ch: 想要「诡计」不败露，至少得先管好嘴巴…你说呢？
t_en: Hey! If you don't want the "Trickery" to be exposed, you'd better start by keeping your mouth shut... What do you say?

t_ch: 呃…桀桀桀，大姐头，您说得太对啦！我一定谨言慎行！
t_en: Uh... hehe, Boss Lady, you're absolutely right! I'll be careful with my words!

t_ch: 这玩意，启动起来动静太大…还是别节外生枝了吧？
t_en: This thing's way too loud when it's on... Let's not stir up more trouble, okay?

t_ch: 你看它的脸——哎呀呀，怎么会有长得这么丑的东西？啧啧……
t_en: Look at its face — oh my, how could something be so ugly? Tsk tsk...

t_ch: 对啊对啊，真丑啊…丑到连我这个没有消化系统的小鬼都觉得反胃！
t_en: Yeah, yeah, so ugly... ugly enough to make even me, a little imp without a digestive system, feel nauseous!

t_ch: 要是让这么丑的家伙弄伤了我的脸蛋，恐怕铁面无私的塔兰顿看了都会忍不住流泪吧？
t_en: If that ugly brute messes up my face, even the stoic Talanton might end up shedding a tear.

t_ch: 可不是嘛，那就太让人伤心了！果然逃跑才是理智的做法，桀桀桀……
t_en: Indeed, that would be heartbreaking! Clearly, running away is the sensible choice, hehe...

t_ch: 所以，干脆就由你在前面扛着吧？我负责在边上补补刀就行了~
t_en: So, how about you take the front? I'll attack from the side.

t_ch: 没错没错，撒腿就跑才符合咱们盗贼的身……
t_en: Right, right. Fleeing fits our rogue nature...

t_ch: 哈！既不中看也不中用的家伙，才两三下就被撂倒了，真没劲。
t_en: Ha! Such an ugly and useless creature, taken down with just a couple of hits. How boring!

t_ch: 喂，巴特鲁斯——还活着吗？活着就吱个声。
t_en: Hey, Bartholos, are you still alive? Give us a squeak if you are.

t_ch: …到！啊不是，吱！
t_en: ...Here! I mean, squeak!

t_ch: 呵，瞧你那怂样，这才多大点场面就遭不住了？
t_en: Look at you, all shaken up. Can't handle even this bit of action?

t_ch: 这里现在应该安全了，快干活吧。
t_en: It should be safe here now. Get to work.

t_ch: 遵命，赛飞儿大姐头！
t_en: Aye aye, Boss Lady Cipher!

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯开启了挖掘模式，找出了藏于地底的宝藏……
t_en: Bartholos activated excavation mode, unearthing the treasure buried deep underground...

t_ch: 嗅…嗅嗅……
t_en: Sniff... sniff...

t_ch: 嗯…这次没有什么特别的味道了，感觉里面没什么值钱的玩意。挖都挖出来了，还是打开看一眼吧。
t_en: Hmm... It doesn't smell like anything valuable this time. Since we've already dug it up, let's open it and see.

t_ch: 嗯…？这是……
t_en: Hmm...? Is this...

t_ch: 哎呀…这看上去可真够惨的。你觉得他们成功逃到奥赫玛了吗？
t_en: Oh... this looks tragic. Do you think they made it to Okhema?

t_ch: 八成没有。我在奥赫玛从没见过斯缇科西亚出身的家伙，连听都没听说过。
t_en: Probably not. I've never seen or heard of anyone from Styxia in Okhema.

t_ch: 多洛斯起码还剩下我这个独苗…这座城想必就没那么幸运了吧？
t_en: At least Dolos still has me left... Guess this city wasn't so lucky, huh?

t_ch: 呵，想想还真是不公平呀。仅仅是因为生错了地方，就得承受这样的灭顶之灾…反倒是那些奥赫玛人，只因为背靠负世泰坦，所以能一直维系到现在……
t_en: Heh, it does seem unfair, doesn't it? Just because they were born in the wrong place, they had to face such... devastation. Meanwhile, those in Okhema, simply because they're supported by the Worldbearing Titan, continue to survive...

t_ch: 可、可不是嘛，这世界真是不公平呀……
t_en: Yeah, the world just isn't fair, is it?

t_ch: 说到负世泰坦，赛飞儿大姐头，你以前是不是和我说起来过…<color=#dbc291ff>你曾经在刻法勒的祭司院当过学徒？</color>
t_en: Speaking of the Worldbearing Titan, Cipher, didn't you tell me once... <color=#dbc291ff>you used to be an apprentice at Kephale's Priestly Court?</color>
...Huh?

t_ch: 我…说过？跟你？
t_en: Did I?

t_ch: 对、对呀！你、你肯定跟我说过，我记得可清楚了！可能时间过了太久，你的记忆也变模糊了吧？桀、桀桀……
t_en: Yes! You definitely told me, and I remember it clearly! Maybe it's been so long, your memory's gotten a little fuzzy, hehe...
Hmph...

t_ch: …啊对，想起来了，我是跟你说过。也没什么大不了的嘛，就是混进了一群神神叨叨的祭司里，天天看着他们在那块发光的大石头前边装神弄鬼。
t_en: ...Ah, right, I did tell you. It wasn't a big deal. I just snuck in with a bunch of mumbling priests and watched them do their spooky rituals around some glowing rock.

t_ch: 老实说，当时我只是惦记着元老院大老爷们的钱包，其它的事印象都不深了——这都过去多久了，得有快一千年了吧？
t_en: Honestly, I was only interested in the wallets of the old grandpas on the Council back then. Don't remember much else — it's been, what, nearly a thousand years?

t_ch: 喔…原来如此。穿着一身祭司袍子的赛飞儿大姐头，还真难想象那画面啊？
t_en: Oh... I see. But you in a priest's robe? Now that's hard to picture.

t_ch: …谁让你想了？再说了，千年前的祭祀长袍，款式和现在根本也是两样。
t_en: ...Who asked you to picture that? Besides, priest robes from a thousand years ago were nothing like the ones today.

t_ch: 说、说的也是，是我想当然了…我就是有点好奇，你当时有没有从那些祭司身上打听到什么，桀桀桀……
t_en: Right, right... I didn't think about that. I was just curious if you ever overheard anything interesting from those priests, hehe...

t_ch: …哼，莫名其妙。走吧，下个地方！
t_en: Hmph. How ridiculous. Come on, let's head to the next spot!

t_ch: 嘁，哪冒出来的路障？
t_en: Tsk, where did this roadblock come from?

t_ch: 真麻烦哪，明明宝贝就在眼前了……
t_en: This is so annoying. The treasure is right in front of us...

t_ch: 哎呀呀，这下卡关咯。不过，我有一计——
t_en: Oh dear, we're stuck here. But I have a plan —

t_ch: 这个时候…要不要求助于又大、又吵、又笨重的「扎格列斯之手」？
t_en: Should we call on that big, noisy, cumbersome Hand of Zagreus?

t_ch: 哈…没想到那玩意还真能派上用场。行吧，你来操作？
t_en: Ha... never thought that thing would actually come in handy. Alright, do you want to handle it?

t_ch: 桀桀桀…交给我吧，大姐头！
t_en: Hehe... leave it to me, Boss!

t_ch: 我可不需要什么控制装置！毕竟…咳，你懂的！
t_en: I don't need a controller! After all... you know!

t_ch: …嘢？怎么突然停下来了？
t_en: ...Eh? Why did it suddenly stop?

t_ch: 咳…出了一丢丢小问题。没关系，再来一次！
t_en: *cough* ...Just a tiny hiccup. No worries. Let's try again.

t_ch: 扎格列斯之手，我的宝贝儿——启动！
t_en: Hand of Zagreus, my darling — activate!

t_ch: 桀桀桀！效果拔群！
t_en: Hehe! Works like a charm!

t_ch: 哈，挺能干的嘛！虽然我还是没看出这玩意和「诡计」有什么关系就是了。
t_en: Ha, not bad! However, I still don't see how this ties into "Trickery."

t_ch: 冲冲冲，下一处宝藏！
t_en: Let's go, to the next trove!

t_ch: 欸，先别急嘛。刚才你问我的那个问题，我好像突然想起来了——
t_en: Hey, slow down. That question you asked me earlier... I think I just remembered something.

t_ch: …嘢？问题，什么问题？
t_en: ...Eh? What did I ask?

t_ch: 就是…那个问题啊。刚刚挖出第二个宝箱以后，你特意问我的。
t_en: You know... that question. The one you specifically asked me, right after we dug up the second chest.

t_ch: 呃…恕我直言，大姐头，你最近是不是精神压力有点大？我刚才哪问过什么问题，你肯定是幻听了！
t_en: Uh... forgive me, Boss Lady, but have you been under a lot of stress lately? I didn't ask anything. You must've imagined it!

t_ch: 哦…这样啊？
t_en: Oh... is that so?

t_ch: 可能还真被你说中了，我最近精神是有点紧绷，可得好好放松下哪…走吧，去下一处宝藏。
t_en: Maybe you're right. I have been a little on edge lately. I need to relax... Alright, let's move on to the next treasure trove.

t_ch: 宝藏！冲冲冲！
t_en: Treasure! Let's go!
(Hmph...)

t_ch: 嗅…嗅嗅……
t_en: Sniff... sniff...

t_ch: 哎呀呀，这气味…感觉这地底下藏着什么不得了的东西哪？
t_en: Ooh, this scent... feels like something incredible is buried here.

t_ch: 不得了，不得了！桀桀桀，大姐头都这么说了，搞得我也跃跃欲试了呀。
t_en: Incredible indeed! Hehe, if even Boss Lady says so, how can I not be excited?

t_ch: 还用我指挥吗？开挖吧。
t_en: Do I need to give instructions here? Get digging!

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯开启了挖掘模式，找出了藏于地底的……
t_en: Bartholos activated excavation mode, uncovering something hidden beneath the earth...
...Heh?

t_ch: 哎呀，这不是「黄金替罪羊」吗？有点意思！看来把宝物埋在这里的家伙，是想跟我们玩个游戏？
t_en: Oh, isn't this a Golden Scapegoat? Interesting! Looks like whoever buried this treasure wanted to play a little game with us.

t_ch: 喔，关于这山羊头是不是还有什么神话来着？「怕火的成为了羊，不怕火的成为了人」……
t_en: Oh, isn't there a myth about this thing? "Those who feared fire became sheep, those who did not became human"...

t_ch: 什么羊不羊人不人的，我只知道把这玩意解开能拿到宝贝。喏，这殊荣就交给你了。
t_en: Sheep, humans — whatever. All I know is unlocking this gets us treasure. Here, this honor's all yours.

t_ch: 小谜题跟小把戏，都在我的舒适区！交给小的我，您尽管放心~
t_en: Puzzles and tricks are right up my alley! Just leave it to little ol' me.

t_ch: 就说难不倒我吧，桀桀桀！
t_en: Told you nothing can stump me, hehe!

t_ch: 赛飞儿大姐头，现在轮到你……
t_en: Cipher, it's your turn now...

t_ch: …停，待在那儿别动，别转过来。
t_en: ...Stop, stay there, and don't turn around.

t_ch: 欸？什么意思……
t_en: Eh? What do you mean...

t_ch: 我都说了，你别乱动！
t_en: I told you, don't move!

t_ch: …哎哟喂！
t_en: ...Ouch!

t_ch: 疼、好疼！我这看不见的千年老腰啊，你干嘛——
t_en: Ow, my poor old back — what are you doing?

t_ch: …嘘，安静！别说多余的话。
t_en: ...Shh, quiet! Don't say anything unnecessary.

t_ch: 那裁缝女——她全都在听着呢。
t_en: That seamstress, she's listening.

t_ch: 什、什么？！大姐头，你的意思是……
t_en: Wh—What!? Boss, do you mean...

t_ch: 你在一边待着，别出声…我来会会她。
t_en: Stay put and keep quiet... I'll handle her.

t_ch: 唷，裁缝女！你的手段越来越卑鄙了，真以为我发现不了你挂在我身边的小耳朵？
t_en: Well, well, seamstress! Getting sneaky, aren't we? Did you think I wouldn't notice your little spies lurking around me?

t_ch: 「伴随着时光的推移…我也开始越发依赖自己的侥幸心理。」
t_en: "As time passes... I find myself leaning more and more on blind luck."

t_ch: 「仅以这小小若虫的力量，果然还是瞒不过你的锐眼啊…赛法利娅。」
t_en: "With only the power of this little nymph, naturally I couldn't slip past your sharp eyes... Cifera."

t_ch: 哼…就算你能完全掌控巴特鲁斯的意识，我照样能戳穿你的伪装——阿格莱雅。
t_en: Hmph... Even if you could fully control Bartholos's mind, I would still be able to see through your disguise — Aglaea.

t_ch: 你费尽心思监听我的一举一动，是什么居心？我早都说了，没有我你们也能成事，何必这么死缠烂打？
t_en: You've gone to such lengths to spy on my every move — what exactly are you after? I've told you before, you don't need me to succeed. Why won't you just let this go?

t_ch: 遐蝶和灰子那一趟，我已经破例出手了。再要狮子大开口——未免有点得寸进尺吧？
t_en: I already made an exception and got involved with Castorice and Gray Mystery. Asking for more now... isn't that pushing your luck a bit too far?

t_ch: 「我…很抱歉，赛法利娅。」
t_en: "I... I'm sorry, Cifera."

t_ch: 「我此生机关算尽，却无论如何也预料不到，你我的关系竟会凝至冰点。」
t_en: "I've plotted and schemed my whole life, but I never could've predicted our relationship would end up like this."

t_ch: 「为了理清我究竟做错了什么…我花了上百年的时间思考、自省；但你始终不愿给我一个当面坦陈的机会。」
t_en: "I've spent centuries trying to figure out where I went wrong, replaying every moment over and over. But you've never given me a chance to explain myself face-to-face."

t_ch: 你…别用那种语气，你知道我最受不了这个。
t_en: Don't... don't talk to me like that. You know I can't take it.

t_ch: 「千年以前，那个无知的我…曾因沉默失去生命中最悦耳的浪花。」
t_en: "A thousand years ago, out of foolishness... I let my silence steal away the most beautiful sound I'd ever known."

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>海瑟音……</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Hysilens...</color>

t_ch: 「所以这一次，即便只是徒劳，我也必须把讯息传入你的耳中——我需要你，赛法利娅。黄金裔的使命需要你。」
t_en: "So this time, I must say this to you, even if it's all in vain — I need you, Cifera. The Chrysos Heirs' mission needs you."

t_ch: 「我那被推迟太久的终幕…总算要到来了。无论在你心中对我存有多少芥蒂，它都将随我的离去云散烟消。」
t_en: "The finale I've delayed for far too long... it's finally here. Whatever resentment you still hold against me will fade once I'm gone."

t_ch: 「回奥赫玛来吧，我请求你。若没有你，他们将无法赢得抗争。」
t_en: "Please, come back to Okhema. I'm begging you. Without you, they don't stand a chance."

t_ch: …嘁。我会考虑的，裁缝女。别再监视我了…除非你想彻底失去我的影踪。
t_en: ...I'll think about it, seamstress. But stop watching me... unless you want to lose me for good.

t_ch: 「呵…放心吧。即便我十分希望能再听见你的声音，再看一眼你的面庞……」
t_en: "Heh... Don't worry. As much as I'd love to hear your voice again, to see your face..."

t_ch: 「…恐怕也不会再有机会了。再见，赛法利娅。」
t_en: "...it seems that chance will never come. Goodbye, Cifera."

t_ch: …别了，阿格莱雅。
t_en: ...Goodbye, Aglaea.

t_ch: 结果…那宝箱里的东西都被冥河水浸透了，没什么实际价值。
t_en: Turns out... everything in that chest was soaked in water from the River of Souls. Nothing valuable left.

t_ch: 啊呀，可惜。我还以为那山羊头底下会藏着什么好宝贝呢。
t_en: Ah, what a shame. I thought there'd be some real treasure under that goat's head.

t_ch: 那些藏品以前确实价值连城。只是遭时间冲刷得太久，就失去了原本的样子。
t_en: Those relics were once priceless. But time has eroded away all that they once were.

t_ch: 喔…从你嘴巴里说出来，感觉好伤感啊？
t_en: Oh... hearing that from you makes it sound so sad.

t_ch: 不对，我感叹这个干嘛！刚才发生的状况，我还没缓过来呢——那个可怕的女人，她到底是什么时候……
t_en: Wait, why am I getting all sentimental!? I haven't even processed what just happened — that horrible woman, when did she...

t_ch: 若你下次遇见赛飞儿，替我转告她：<color=#dbc291ff>身为半神，我们永远不可能逃避职责。</color>
t_en: If you see her, let her know this: <color=#dbc291ff>As demigods, we can't avoid our duties forever.</color>

t_ch: 唔，难道就是那个时候……
t_en: Hmm... could it have been back then when...

t_ch: 想起来了？看来用不着我来帮你回忆了哪。她肯定是趁你大意，偷偷把若虫塞进了你的「口袋」里。
t_en: Do you remember now? Looks like you don't need me to jog your memory. She must've slipped that nymph into your "pocket" while you were distracted.

t_ch: 别小看那虫子，它跟裁缝女的金线一样神通广大。你稍有松懈就有可能被它接管，像操纵傀儡一样——然后，你就会随口蹦出些平时绝不会说的字句。
t_en: Don't underestimate that bug. It's every bit as dangerous as the seamstress's golden thread. Let your guard down for even a second, and it might take over — make you spill secrets you'd never share otherwise.

t_ch: 哇，简直就像…简直就像从黑潮里滋生的洗脑蚀怪一样，太吓人啦！
t_en: Whoa, that's like... some brainwashing monster straight out of the black tide. Creepy!

t_ch: 那…赛飞儿大姐头，你是怎么发现异常的？我有点好奇，那小虫到底操纵着我说了什么怪话？
t_en: Boss, how did you realize something was wrong? I'm curious — what exactly did that nymph make me say?

t_ch: 哼，其实它早就露馅了……
t_en: Hmph, actually, it gave itself away very early on...

t_ch: 说到负世泰坦，赛飞儿大姐头，你以前是不是和我说起来过…你曾经在刻法勒的祭司院当过学徒？
t_en: Speaking of the Worldbearing Titan, Cipher, didn't you tell me once... you used to be an apprentice at Kephale's Priestly Court?

t_ch: 咱俩搭伙作案一千年，能相互分享的话题早就说尽了。唯独那段在黎明云崖的经历，我不曾跟你聊过。
t_en: We've been partners in crime for a thousand years. We've talked about everything there is to talk about. Except for that one experience at Dawncloud. I never told you about that.

t_ch: 我绝对…<color=#dbc291ff>绝对不可能和任何人提及那事。</color>
t_en: I... <color=#dbc291ff>absolutely wouldn't have shared that with anyone.</color>

t_ch: 不过，呃…大姐头，也有一种可能，阿格莱雅不是故意想要骗你的……
t_en: But, uh... Boss Lady, there's a chance Aglaea wasn't trying to trick you...

t_ch: 我印象里，「诡计」的化身对逼人吐露真言的金线深恶痛绝。你绝对不可能替阿格莱雅辩护……
t_en: And as far as I know, the embodiment of "Trickery" despises that golden thread, which forces people to speak the truth. You'd never defend Aglaea...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>对吧，{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Isn't that right, {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#}?</color>

t_ch: 你…还真是机警得可怕呀，桀桀桀……
t_en: You... have a terrifyingly sharp mind, hehe...

t_ch: 毕竟，这世上知道你还活着的人就只有我一个嘛。为了让你活过泰坦试炼，我可是设计骗过了全世界哪。
t_en: After all, I'm the only one in the world who knows you're still alive. To make sure you survived the Titan trial, I had to fool the entire world.

t_ch: 如果不想让谎言被戳破…你未来也得好好表现哦？
t_en: If you want to keep that lie from falling apart... you'd better keep playing your part in the days ahead.

t_ch: 哪怕不是为了我这条不值钱的小命，我也甘心为赛飞儿大姐头赴汤蹈火呀！
t_en: Even if it weren't for saving my worthless little life, I'd still walk through fire for Cipher without hesitation!

t_ch: 哼，你被缩减到这幅模样以后，唯一还没退化的就是说话的艺术了。
t_en: Hmph, you may have been reduced to this form, but you still haven't lost your way with words.

t_ch: 咳…话说回来，大姐头…那个黑心女人说的话，你应该不会放在心上吧？
t_en: *cough* ...Speaking of which, Cipher... you're not taking that wicked woman's words to heart, are you?

t_ch: 咱们这消闲日子过得多快活呀，何必非要趟这趟浑水呢…你说对吧？
t_en: Life's been pretty great lately. Why get into trouble now... Don't you think?

t_ch: 老实说，我在想……
t_en: Honestly, I've been thinking...

t_ch: 也许现在回去一趟也无妨。
t_en: Maybe a trip back now wouldn't be too bad.

t_ch: …你又回来了，赛——
t_en: ...You're back again, Cif—
—Ah.

t_ch: 赛法利娅，你的脸上……
t_en: Cifera, your face...

t_ch: …我需要衣服。
t_en: ...I need clothes.

t_ch: 随便，随便丢给我一件衣服。一块布也行，能遮住伤口就够了。
t_en: Anything, just throw me something. A piece of cloth will do, just enough to cover the wounds.

t_ch: 欠下的钱，我会还给你…我保证。
t_en: I will pay back the amount I owe... I promise.

t_ch: 你…流着金黄色的血呢。
t_en: You... you're bleeding golden blood.

t_ch: …是，那又怎么样？
t_en: ...Yes, what about it?

t_ch: 对，我是他们嘴里说的黄金裔——力大无穷的英雄！名扬天下的剑客！智慧超群的大学者！
t_en: So what if I'm one of those Chrysos Heirs they talk about? Those infinitely strong heroes? World-renowned swordsmen? Exceptionally intelligent scholars!?

t_ch: 赛法利娅……
t_en: Cifera...

t_ch: 真是不好意思哪，那么厉害的一群家伙里，居然也会出现我这种穷人、小偷、骗子！不如我在身上再划一道口子，把这黄色的玩意全都放干……
t_en: Well, I am sorry to disappoint! Who knew that group of amazing people would include me! A pauper, thief, con artist! Maybe I should just make another cut to drain all this yellow stuff out of me and-

t_ch: …别说了，赛法利娅。
t_en: ...Stop it, Cifera.

t_ch: 我会给你衣服，帮你暖和起来。然后……
t_en: I'll give you clothes, warm you up, and...

t_ch: 然后…留下来吧，留在我的织坊，直到你的伤势痊愈。
t_en: Stay here. Stay in my workshop until you're healed.
...Tch.

t_ch: 你不打算问我吗？问我这些伤是怎么落下的？
t_en: Aren't you going to ask me how I got these wounds?

t_ch: 呵，也对，问了又怎么样？我那么擅长撒谎，连你的金线都能骗过——既然确认不了真假，又何必多嘴一问呢？
t_en: Heh, right, what's the use? I'm so good at lying, even your golden thread can be fooled. If you can't verify the truth, why bother asking at all?

t_ch: …我不需要考问，也无需动用金线度量。我知道你所做的一切都是为了生存，不计代价。
t_en: ...I don't need to ask, nor do I need to use the golden thread. I know everything you do is for survival, at all costs.

t_ch: 留在这里吧。在这里，你不必再忍受饥寒交迫。在我身边，你可以学着缝补已经破碎的自尊。
t_en: Stay here. You won't have to deal with the cold or go hungry again. Stay by my side, and you can start putting yourself back together.

t_ch: 你…不怕我会给这里带来坏名声？你不怕我偷偷顺走你贵重的衣服，拿去外面倒卖？
t_en: Aren't you... worried I'll ruin this place's reputation? Or that I might sneak off with your precious clothes and sell them?

t_ch: 若是畏惧这些，我便不会向你发出邀请。
t_en: I wouldn't have invited you if I were worried about those things.

t_ch: 裁缝女，你真怪。大人们常说，经常撒谎的孩子本性难移……
t_en: You're weird, seamstress. You know, they say a child who often lies remains a liar for life...

t_ch: …我努力试过不去活成他们口中的样子。但讲真的？那太累了…我早就想放弃了。
t_en: I've tried not to become a liar. But... honestly? It's just too hard... I'm close to giving up.

t_ch: 你凭什么觉得我不会让你失望？
t_en: What makes you think I won't disappoint you?

t_ch: 因为我能感觉得到——并非依靠半神的力量，而是凭着在我胸口跳动的心脏——你喜爱这个地方。
t_en: Because I can feel it — not with the power of a demigod, but with the heart that beats in my chest — you love this place.

t_ch: 所以你才会频繁经过。所以你才会羞于停留，因为你害怕自己配不上「金织」的招牌。
t_en: That's why you keep coming by but also why you hesitate to stay longer. You worry you'll tarnish the "Goldweaver" name.

t_ch: 接过墨涅塔的神职以后，我已经习惯了让金线替我分辨真诚和虚假。但我想要完成的那份使命，它偏偏需要我学会用心灵去信任、依赖。
t_en: After taking over Mnestia's divine authority, I became used to letting the golden thread distinguish sincerity from falsehood. However, the mission I need to complete requires me to learn to trust and rely on my heart.

t_ch: 赛法利娅——你是我交给这世界的一颗真心…也是它向我发起的一次挑战。
t_en: Cifera — you're an honest thing I've given to this world... and also a challenge the world has given to me.

t_ch: 留在我身边吧。你有一张美丽的脸孔，它不该常与伤痕跟淤泥作伴。
t_en: Stay here with me. You have a beautiful face that deserves better than to be covered in scars and grime.

t_ch: 你说话可真是九折十八弯，让人摸不着头脑哪。
t_en: You've got a way of talking that's hard to follow.

t_ch: 那我们就…约定好了。给你一天机会反悔——等践行时一过，再想赶我走可就晚了！
t_en: Alright, let's make it a deal. You have one day to change your mind — once the Action Hour passes, there's no turning back.

t_ch: 话说…我不在的时候，你都是靠什么法子寻宝的啊，大姐头？
t_en: Tell me... how did you hunt for treasure when I wasn't around, Boss Lady?

t_ch: 哼，想套我的话？门儿都没有。
t_en: Hmph, trying to trick me into saying something? Not a chance.

t_ch: 桀桀桀，你可真机警呀……
t_en: Hehe, you're as sharp as ever...

t_ch: 呼…这感觉，还真神奇…你什么时候学会的这招？
t_en: Huh... this feels truly magical... When did you pick up this trick?

t_ch: 呃…自学成才，纯粹自学成才！
t_en: Uh... self-taught, all self-taught!

t_ch: 过来了！把这大射灯关了吧？还怪刺眼的。
t_en: We're here! Can we turn off this spotlight now? It's really blinding.

t_ch: 没问题！用那边的装置把它关了就行。
t_en: No problem! Just use the device over there to shut it off.

t_ch: 嗅…嗅嗅……
t_en: Sniff... sniff...

t_ch: 是金币、宝石还有上等皮革的香气…叫人心情舒畅！
t_en: The scent of gold coins, gems, and fine leather... it's so refreshing!

t_ch: 跑、跑慢点，大姐头！
t_en: Slow down, Boss!

t_ch: 你腿长、我腿短！
t_en: Your legs are long and mine are short!

t_ch: 大姐头，这回咱们指定赚得盆满钵满，不知你打算怎么消费？
t_en: Boss Lady, we're set to make a fortune this time. Got any plans on how to spend it?

t_ch: 当然是——分文不花。我那个愚蠢的预言，你忘了？
t_en: Of course — spend nothing. Have you forgotten my silly prophecy?

t_ch: 啊…记得，当然记得！桀桀桀……
t_en: Ah... I remember, of course! Hehehe...

t_ch: 哎呀！那不是我…
t_en: Oh! Isn't that my...

t_ch: …嗯哼？
t_en: ...Uh-huh?

t_ch: …咳，那不是扎格列斯之手的控制装置嘛？
t_en: ...Ahem, isn't that a controller for the Hand of Zagreus?

t_ch: 嚯，可不是嘛。
t_en: Ho, indeed it is.

t_ch: 嚯，这里还守着个大家伙哪？
t_en: Ho, there's a big guy guarding this place!

t_ch: 是啊是啊…又大又呆，纯纯一坨傻大个！
t_en: Yeah, yeah... big and dumb, just a pure lump of stupidity!

t_ch: 快快快，冲冲冲~
t_en: Here we go round the River of Souls~

t_ch: 两脚贼灵在行动~
t_en: Spirithief goes, Spirithief goes~

t_ch: …你唱的什么乱七八糟的，根本不在调上吧？
t_en: ...What are you even singing? You're totally out of tune!

t_ch: 熊熊火种在胸中凝结~胸中凝结~凝结！
t_en: Twinkle, twinkle, in my chest~ How this Coreflame is the best!

t_ch: 论歌喉，我还是更胜一筹呀，大姐头！
t_en: When it comes to singing, I still sound better than you, Boss!

t_ch: …超越时~空~逐火旅~途~
t_en: ...Go beyond all time and space~ On the Journey of Flame-Chase~

t_ch: 这群黑衣人……
t_en: These folks in black...

t_ch: 既不回应挑衅，看上去也不打算动武——那他们究竟在等什么？
t_en: Are not responding to provocations, nor do they seem intent on fighting. What exactly are they waiting for?

t_ch: 施压。制造不安。植入焦虑。看来，这是他们所选择的策略。
t_en: Pressure. Fear. Planting seeds of anxiety. It seems this is the strategy they've chosen.

t_ch: 将抹杀黄金裔作为使命的组织……
t_en: An organization that sees it as their mission to eradicate the Chrysos Heirs...
